source: /tmp/src_2022ND158.txt (lines 167, showing spaced ellipsis with 3-space gaps between dots)
evidence: The DB text contains '. . . .' (4 periods with single spaces between them) at position 7387 in paragraph [¶10], immediately after 'compelling' and before 'The Court finds that PKI's purchase'. The authoritative PDF source shows '.   .   .' (3 periods with 3-space gaps between them) on line 167 of the source file. This is the standard spacing used in the printed opinion for an ellipsis indicating an omission from the quoted court finding.

PDF shows the citation parenthetical on the same line as the reporter: 'See In re S.R.B., 2013 ND 109, ¶ 7, 832 N.W.2d 42 (the more probing clearly'. The DB text incorrectly splits this with a newline before the opening parenthesis. This breaks the citation across lines unnaturally.
note: Citation parenthetical was incorrectly broken by newline during ingestion/processing. The parenthetical '(the more probing clearly...)' belongs immediately after the page number with only a space.
